title: Quantization tradeoffs for serving
description: Lower-precision weights/activations (int8, fp8) cut memory and raise throughput, but can shift accuracy, so serving quality must be re-validated.
body: Use when memory or throughput is the serving bottleneck. Quantizing to int8/fp8 shrinks the model and KV cache and speeds compute on supporting hardware, raising the batch you can fit — but it can degrade accuracy, sometimes only on certain inputs. Do not assume quantization is lossless. Actions: quantize, then re-run the evaluation set and compare quality to the full-precision baseline before serving. Validation signals include memory/throughput gains with quality within an accepted tolerance. Risk boundary: serving a quantized model without re-validating quality can ship a silent accuracy regression; treat quantization as a model change requiring evaluation.
